title: GRB 250702B and 3I/ATLAS Alignment analysis

definitions.definition: Coordinate comparison between gamma-ray burst and interstellar object

definitions.term: NO - They did not account for 3I/ATLAS being potentially in or near the line of sight.
definitions.definition: The GRB 250702B was detected on July 2, 2025, and 3I/ATLAS was discovered on July 1, 2025 - **one day before the GRB**.

definitions.term: approximately the same region of sky
The objects were in NEIGHBORING constellations
GRB was at eastern edge of Scutum
definitions.definition: (RA 18h 59m), essentially on the border with Sagittarius.

definitions.definition: - Both unprecedented in their own ways

- Swift X-Ray Telescope provides precise GRB location
> “Stacking of the WXT data taken prior to the WXT detection indicated that **the source has already emerged on July 1st, 2025**.”

definitions.term: The GRB showed X-ray activity on July 1, the same day 3I/ATLAS was discovered.

definitions.definition: - Known data (3I/ATLAS composition, position, discovery date): **IGNORED**

definitions.term: Check if path intersects 3I/ATLAS plasma cavity

If a nickel-rich interstellar object with unprecedented composition crosses the heliospheric boundary on July 1, and the longest gamma-ray burst ever recorded occurs on July 2 in the same region of sky with X-ray emission starting July 1…
definitions.definition: **…shouldn’t someone at least CHECK if they’re related?**

*The variables left out of the black hole model include: the entire solar system, its plasma environment, objects within it, and the possibility that not everything detected from space originates from cosmological distances.*
